name: UNIVERSITY OF SOUTH DAKOTA RESIDENT DINING

violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Food employee was observed removing items from the dish machine upon entering the dish room without first washing his hands. Hands should be washed before touching clean dishes.

General employee practices. — --Food employee observed eating while preparing food and outside of designated area. Observed food employee eating food on the serving line. Food should only be eaten in designated areas, away from serving and prep areas.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Surface of equipment/utensil did not reach minimum temperature of 160F during hot water sanitize rinse of warewash machine. The maximum temperature reached on inspection thermometer was 133F after running through the dish machine twice. Dish machines that use heat for sanitizing must be able to heat the surfaces of equipment to 160F.

Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Food items found on the floor of roll-in refrigerator used in the Simple Servings area. Food items should be stored at least 6 inches off of the floor.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Shelving units in the produce walk-in cooler were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Hood ventilation system filters were found dirty. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.
